categories.content:
Welcome to the SONiC Landscape

SONiC (Software for Open Networking in the Cloud) is an open-source network operating system (NOS) originally created by Microsoft for its Azure data centers and open-sourced in 2016. Today it is a Linux Foundation project governed by the SONiC Foundation.

SONiC decouples network software from the underlying hardware, running on hundreds of switch platforms from multiple vendors while supporting a full suite of routing and switching features including BGP, EVPN/VXLAN, RDMA, QoS, and more. Its modular, container-based architecture makes it cloud- native by design and readily automatable.

This landscape provides a comprehensive view of the companies, products, and hardware platforms that make up the SONiC ecosystem — from Premier Members who shape the project's direction to specialist vendors delivering production-ready SONiC distributions, orchestration tools, and validated white-box hardware.

SONiC Foundation Membership

The SONiC Foundation comprises three membership tiers:

Premier Members

Premier Members are the strategic driving force of the SONiC Foundation. They include hyperscalers (Alibaba, Google, Microsoft), silicon vendors (Broadcom, Marvell, NVIDIA), networking incumbents (Arista, Cisco, Dell, Nokia), and AI-era newcomers (Nexthop AI, Upscale AI, Celestica). Premier Members have the broadest governance rights and resource commitments.

General Members

General Members form a diverse ecosystem of ODMs, software companies, cloud providers, and specialist vendors. They include open-networking pioneers such as Aviz Networks, Edgecore Networks, Hedgehog, PANTHEON.tech, PLVision, and STORDIS — each contributing unique products, services, and expertise that extend SONiC into enterprise and edge markets.

Associate Members

Associate Members are academic institutions and non-profit organizations advancing SONiC research, education, and sovereign/government use cases. They include EPFL, University of Rome Tor Vergata, CMKL University, BDBOS, and the Abater Foundation.

subcategories.content: Premier Members hold the highest level of membership, providing significant resources and leadership to the SONiC Foundation. They include hyperscalers, silicon vendors, and major networking vendors who use SONiC at scale and drive its technical roadmap.

General Members contribute products, services, and expertise that broaden SONiC's reach from hyperscaler to enterprise markets. Many General Members ship validated SONiC distributions, orchestration platforms, and open white-box hardware that make SONiC production-ready for organizations of all sizes.

Associate Members include academic institutions and non-profit organizations that advance SONiC research, train the next generation of open-networking engineers, and explore SONiC in government and sovereign infrastructure contexts.

categories.content:
SONiC Products & Solutions

The SONiC ecosystem has spawned a rich set of commercial and open-source products spanning orchestration, NOS distributions, observability, testing, and professional services. This category showcases the key solutions built by SONiC Foundation members.

subcategories.content:
Network orchestration tools manage the entire lifecycle of SONiC fabrics — from intent definition and device onboarding through Day 2+ operations. Key solutions include:

SandWork (PANTHEON.tech) — Intent-based orchestration and automation purpose-built for enterprise SONiC, supporting VXLAN, MC-LAG, multi-vendor fabrics, and both open-source and Enterprise SONiC distributions.

Aviz ONES — A vendor-agnostic operations suite providing orchestration, deep observability, and real-time monitoring for any SONiC deployment with enterprise SLAs.

Hedgehog Open Network Fabric — A Kubernetes-native, open-source fabric platform that turns SONiC into a programmable cloud-like network via VPC abstractions and Kubernetes CRDs.

Several members build hardened, supported distributions of SONiC that fill the gap between raw open-source community code and production requirements:

Aviz Certified Community SONiC — Multi-ASIC, hardware-agnostic, enterprise-ready distribution with 500+ automated tests and 24/7 support. Positioned as the "Red Hat for networking."

Enterprise SONiC (STORDIS) — Broadcom-based ruggedised distribution with feature enrichment for data center, campus, and telecom, paired with MONSOON telemetry and Open2Support SLAs.

SONiC Lite (PLVision) — Lightweight distribution designed for access, edge, and OOB management switches running on 30–50% less capable (and less expensive) hardware than Community SONiC requires.

Visibility into SONiC networks is critical for production operations. The ecosystem provides purpose-built tools including STORDIS MONSOON, Aviz Network Copilot™ (AI-powered assistant), and Aviz Packet Broker for deep flow observability.

Quality and interoperability testing ensures reliable SONiC deployments. Aviz FTAS provides 500+ automated test cases across multi-vendor hardware, while PLVision SAI Challenger offers an open-source framework for SAI API testing used by silicon vendors and ODMs worldwide.

For organizations that want to build SONiC capability without hiring a full in-house team, several members offer expert engineering services: PANTHEON.tech for NOS development and integration, PLVision for custom distribution builds and SAI work, and STORDIS for training and certification.

categories.content:
SONiC Hardware Ecosystem

SONiC runs on white-box ("bare-metal") switches from a wide range of ODMs and OEMs. The hardware ecosystem is characterized by:

  • Open ASICs: Broadcom Trident/Tomahawk, NVIDIA Spectrum, Marvell Prestera/Teralynx, and Cisco Silicon One all have SONiC SAI support.
  • Validated hardware lists (HCLs): Distributions like SandWork, Aviz ONES, STORDIS Enterprise SONiC, and PLVision SONiC Lite each publish vendor- and model-specific compatibility matrices.
  • Form factor range: From 1G campus PoE switches through 100G leaf, 400G spine, to 800G AI-fabric platforms.

Key hardware providers include Celestica (DS/ES series), Dell (PowerSwitch S/Z/N), Edgecore (ECS/AS series), and Micas Networks.
